food_name: Mamee Monster Noodle Snack Chicken Flavour (1 pack, 25g, eaten dry)

local_names.hindi: मामी मॉन्स्टर नूडल स्नैक चिकन फ्लेवर

aliases: मामी मॉन्स्टर नूडल स्नैक चिकन फ्लेवर

hindi: मामी मॉन्स्टर नूडल स्नैक चिकन फ्लेवर

region_of_origin: Malaysia (manufactured by Mamee-Double Decker Sdn Bhd); widely consumed in Singapore
food_subgroup: Dry instant noodle snack — fried noodle block eaten uncooked as a snack, not prepared with water. 1 pack = 25g.

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not cover branded snack noodle products. Closest parent would be 'instant noodle product' (FOODON:03315595) but no exact match exists for dry-eaten branded snack noodles.
ontology.foodex2_description: Instant noodles — closest FoodEx2 category for fried instant noodle snack products eaten dry. Branded product; exact code unavailable; closest_parent applied.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A039S covers instant noodles broadly; no sub-code exists for dry-eaten branded snack noodles.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for branded dry-eaten noodle snack products.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard pack of Mamee Monster Noodle Snack weighs 25g and is typically consumed in its entirety as a single snack serving, eaten dry without any preparation.

nutrient_source.title: Mamee Monster Noodle Snack Chicken Flavour — Nutrition Information Panel (NIP), Singapore product label
nutrient_source.organization: Mamee-Double Decker Sdn Bhd — Singapore product NIP (USDA Branded Food cross-referenced for micronutrient estimation)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary nutrient values sourced directly from the Mamee Monster Chicken Flavour Singapore product Nutrition Information Panel (NIP). Micronutrients not declared on NIP (monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, water) set to -1 where not available. All NIP-declared values are used as-is at confidence 1.0.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Instant Noodles — analogous estimate based on fried wheat-flour instant noodle products
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database (analogous estimate); Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Mamee Monster Noodle Snack eaten dry. GI ~72 (high) is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for fried wheat-flour instant noodles (GI range 67–76 reported for similar products). The dry-eating format (no water added) does not materially alter the starch gelatinisation state relative to the fried noodle block, supporting use of the fried instant noodle GI analogue. GL per 25g serving = (72 × 16.5g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 11.9. Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content: 1,400mg per 100g; 350mg per 25g pack
hypertension.guidance: At 350mg sodium per 25g pack, a single serving provides 17–35% of the recommended daily sodium limit for adults (2,000mg) and a proportionally higher share for children (1,000–1,500mg/day); individuals with hypertension or hypertension risk should strictly limit consumption to no more than one pack per day and avoid combining with other high-sodium foods.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~72) and moderate glycemic load (~12 per 25g pack) from fried refined wheat starch
type2_diabetes.guidance: The GI of ~72 classifies this snack as high-GI; while a single 25g pack produces a moderate glycemic load of ~12, multi-pack consumption (common in children) rapidly accumulates glycemic load and should be avoided by individuals managing blood glucose; portion control to one pack maximum is advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (9.2g/100g from palm oil frying) combined with very high sodium (1,400mg/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of high saturated fat from palm oil frying (9.2g/100g, representing ~44% of total fat) and very high sodium (1,400mg/100g) makes this snack unfavourable for cardiovascular health; individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption and treat this as an occasional indulgence only.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High saturated fat from palm oil; no anti-inflammatory nutrients present
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: High palm oil-derived saturated fat content and absence of anti-inflammatory nutrients (omega-3, antioxidant vitamins) make this snack nutritionally unsuitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis seeking an anti-inflammatory diet; limit consumption.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mamee Monster Noodle Snack Chicken Flavour is one of Singapore's and Malaysia's most iconic childhood snack foods — a 25g individually wrapped pack of crumbly, deep-fried wheat noodle pieces seasoned with chicken-flavoured powder, eaten entirely dry without any cooking or water preparation. Manufactured by Mamee-Double Decker Sdn Bhd (Malaysia) since 1969, the product is distinguished by its 'Monster' mascot branding and the childhood ritual of shaking the seasoning sachet into the noodle pack before eating. The noodle block is fried in palm oil, giving it a crispy, crumbly texture with a savoury, slightly salty chicken flavour. Mamee Monster is a staple in Singapore primary school tuck shops, convenience stores (7-Eleven, Cheers), and provision shops, and is consumed across all ethnic groups as a nostalgic snack. Clinically, it is an ultra-processed, high-sodium (1,400mg/100g), high-saturated-fat (9.2g/100g from palm oil), high-GI (GI ~72) snack with minimal micronutrient value. A single 25g pack provides approximately 121 kcal, 350mg sodium, 5.2g fat, and 16.5g available carbohydrate. Multi-pack consumption is common among children, rapidly compounding sodium and glycemic load. Singapore's Health Promotion Board has specifically targeted school tuck shop snacks including Mamee Monster in the National Nutrition Survey and Healthier Choice school canteen programmes. The product is halal-certified and dairy-free.

confidence_reason: Primary nutrient values sourced directly from the Mamee Monster Chicken Flavour Singapore product Nutrition Information Panel (NIP) at confidence 1.0. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous fried instant noodle entry at confidence 0.9. All mandatory multilingual names present. Ontology anchored to FoodEx2 closest parent with documented gap note.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not declared on the Singapore product NIP and not available in USDA Branded Food for this exact product; closest analogue data not sufficient to assign specific values with confidence. water_g set to -1: moisture content not declared on NIP and not available in approved sources for this exact fried noodle snack product. trans_fat_g is 0.0 as confirmed zero per product NIP declaration. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 as confirmed zero per product NIP declaration. vitamin_c_mg and vitamin_a_mcg_rae are 0.0 as confirmed zero per product NIP. vitamin_b12_mcg and vitamin_d_mcg are 0.0 as confirmed zero per product NIP. GI of 72 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database fried instant noodle entries; no direct GI measurement for Mamee Monster eaten dry exists in published literature. GL per serving = (72 × 16.5) / 100 = 11.88, rounded to 11.9. Sodium per 25g serving = 350mg. This product is manufactured in Malaysia and distributed in Singapore; the Singapore NIP is the primary label source.
